articles.title: UN warns one million women and girls lose access to support amid US aid cuts
articles.summary: UN warns one million women and girls lose access to support amid US aid cuts The United Nations Women agency warned on Friday that a collapse in funding for women’s organisations at a time when needs are soaring could leave at least one million women and girls without access to vital support. This comes after US President Donald Trump slashed foreign aid after taking office, leading to women in places such as Afghanistan, Gaza, the Democratic Republic of Congo, Sudan and Yemen where needs are acute being deprived of adequate support. As a result, "at least one million women and girls affected

articles.title: Israeli army says it killed Hamas commander in Gaza strike
articles.summary: Israeli army says it killed Hamas commander in Gaza strike Israel’s military said it killed a Hamas commander in a strike on southern Gaza on Thursday. In a statement, the army said the attack targeted Yahya Saeed Muhammad Hamdan, head of the Qassam Brigades Nukhba Forces unit, who allegedly took part in the 7 October 2023 attacks on Israel. There has been no immediate statement from Hamas, which recently said that its spokesperson Hazem Qassem survived an Israeli attack on Thursday west of Gaza City.

articles.title: Smotrich: Steve Witkoff called Gaza Palestinians ‘two million Nazis’
articles.summary: Smotrich: Steve Witkoff called Gaza Palestinians ‘two million Nazis’ Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich has said US envoy Steve Witkoff described Gaza’s entire population as “two million Nazis” during a private meeting last year. Speaking at a conference in Israel on Thursday, the far-right minister recounted a conversation held after Witkoff’s visited Gaza last August at the height of the Israeli genocide. Smotrich said: "When Witkoff was appointed, I sat with him and Ron Dermer [former Israeli ambassador to the US]." They showed Witkoff a propaganda video, after which the US envo

articles.title: Inflation Is a Tailwind for European, Morgan Stanley Says
articles.summary: European equities stand to benefit from higher inflation, according to Morgan Stanley’s Marina Zavolock, who says the market underestimates the region’s exposure to sectors that profit from rising prices. "We have a lot of real asset exposure, we have banks exposure,” Zavolock, the firm’s chief European equity strategist, tells Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

One of the largest settler roads in the West Bank, Route 60 lies east of Ramallah and south of Nablus and its completion requires the appropriation of Palestinian land along its route. articles.summary: Katie King, cofounder and CEO of BioOrbit, discussed the company's recent milestone in producing protein crystals in space. King spoke to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Europe Must Increase Space Autonomy: ESA's Aschbacher
articles.summary: The man who oversees Europe's space agenda says the continent needs to do much more as it pursues greater autonomy in orbit. Europe is trailing the US in efforts to develop a space economy, underscored by SpaceX's mega IPO in June. Josef Aschbacher, Director General of the European Space Agency, spoke to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Greece cuts fuel prices as Iran war strains household budgets
articles.summary: Greece cuts fuel prices as Iran war strains household budgets Greece has announced temporary cuts to petrol and diesel prices as the US-Israeli war on Iran drives up costs across Europe. Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis told parliament on Friday that the country’s two main oil refineries had agreed to reduce prices until the end of August. “Fuel prices will drop by 10 cents per litre and diesel fuel will drop by 5 cents per litre,” he said. The move follows growing pressure on household budgets as the war disrupts energy markets and pushes up transport and living costs across the continent.

articles.title: Elon Musk is an Inspiration: The Exploration Company's Huby

articles.title: Energy agency expects global oil demand to fall in 2026
articles.summary: Energy agency expects global oil demand to fall in 2026 Global oil demand will fall by about one million barrels a day in 2026 as the war between the United States and Iran weighs on the world economy, the International Energy Agency has said. The agency warned that the fighting was creating fresh uncertainty for consumers, businesses and energy markets. Higher risks around shipping and supply could weaken demand further if the conflict escalates. Oil production rose by 4.1 million barrels a day in June as some supplies returned to the market. articles.summary: Tankers transit Hormuz despite renewed US-Iran tensions Vessels transporting liquified natural gas (LNG) have resumed passage through the Strait of Hormuz in recent days, according to ship-tracking data, with authorities in Tokyo reporting that 22 Japan-linked ships have left the Gulf since Tuesday. Despite renewed fighting between the US and Iran, data from Kpler and LSEG has showed at least five LNG tankers entering the strait, including from China, Greece and Qatar.
